'''Gene Ormsby''' is a former English [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He plays on the [[Wing (rugby union)|wing]].

Ormsby joined Warrington at the age of 16 from amateur side [[Oldham St Annes A.R.L.F.C.|Oldham St Annes]] [[Oldham St Annes ARLFC|ARLFC]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Bysouth|first1=Alex|title=Producing good Genes: Ormsby has the Wolves DNA|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/11300272.Producing_good_Genes__Ormsby_has_the_Wolves_DNA|website=Warrington Guardian|accessdate=26 July 2014|date=26 June 2014}}</ref> After spending time as a dual registered player at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], he made his Super League début in February 2014 against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. Ormsby enjoyed a successful opening season with the Wolves, scoring 12 tries in his opening season. He scored his first career hat trick against the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in June 2015 in a 34–18 win.{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}. He has also had a loan spell at [[Oldham RLFC]].

In 2016, he was called up to the initial [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].;<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref> however, he did not make the cut for the final 22-man squad.
